free_text: Spectroscopy of O-16 using alpha+C-12 resonant
scattering in inverse kinematics.
ISMRA and University of Caen.
A He gas target at several pressures was used.
The vacuum chamber,filled with He-4 gas, was isolated
from the beam-line by a 5 mum Havar window. The
thickness of the window was measured using energy loss
calculations by measuring elastic scattering from a
Au-197 target with and without the window in place.
.An R-matrix analysis.
The spins of 8 resonances between 14.1 and 18.5 MeV
were obtained, confirming spin assignments made using
elastic scattering in normal kinematics.
The He-4 decay products were detected
using an array of silicon detectors. Four detector
locations were used- silicon double-sided strip
detector(DSSD).The DSSD's comprised of 32 independent
3 mm wide silicon strips with 16 strips in the
horizontal direction and 16 strips in the vertical
direction. The detectors were calibrated using an alpha
source and elastically scattered C-12 nuclei from 40
mug/cm**2 C-12 and 150 mug/cm**2 Au-197 targets.
The delta-E detectors were calibrated with scattered
beam independently from the 500 mum DSSD's. The energy
resolution of the 500 mum DSSD's was measured to be
150 keV(FWHM) for 5.5 MeV alpha-particles and 230 keV
(FWHM) for the 65 mum detectors.
the tandem Van de Graaff accelerator
at IPN Orsay, France.
*By authors*.The gas pressure was varied between 100
mbar and 510 mbar so that different excitation energy
ranges could be observed at each beam energy.
